prerequisites: An active Luma (lu.ma) account that hosts or manages a calendar.
An active Luma Plus subscription on the calendar you want to access, which is required to use the Luma API.
A Luma API key generated from the calendar's developer settings (provided as the required api_key field).

authMethods.name: API Key
authMethods.summary: Luma authenticates with API key authentication. Pass your key in the x-luma-api-key HTTP request header. This maps to the connector's single required secret field api_key. Keys are scoped per calendar and require an active Luma Plus subscription.

setupSteps.title: Confirm a Luma Plus subscription
setupSteps.body: API access requires an active Luma Plus subscription on the calendar you want to read. Verify the calendar has Luma Plus before generating a key.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key in Luma
setupSteps.body: Go to the Calendars Home in Luma, select your calendar, then open Settings and navigate to the Developer section. In the API Keys area, create or copy your API key. The key is tied to that specific calendar.

setupSteps.title: Provide the key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied key as the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends it in the x-luma-api-key header to authenticate requests. Note Luma's rate limits (200 requests per minute per calendar for calendar keys; 500 requests per minute per organization for organization-level keys), which return a 429 response when exceeded.

sources.title: Luma Help Center - Luma API (plan requirement, API key location, rate limits)
Nango Docs - Luma (x-luma-api-key API key authentication)
